page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core and memory-intensive operations. The server achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, reaching 435.26 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 217.09 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores also rank in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and gzip compression single-threaded benchmarks falling into the average category.

While the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, it shows distinct performance tradeoffs in machine learning tasks. LLM text generation for small models is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, though text generation for medium and large models achieves strong relative performance. Given the high memory bandwidth and dedicated core allocation, this server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, offering resource density and architectural efficiency for ARM-native deployments.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps with no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 435.26 GB/sec and strong multi-threaded decompression. However, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are weaker, representing a clear tradeoff. This instance is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network speed, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server featuring dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and 100 Gbps networking for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on ARMv9 architecture.
192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier large-block read/write bandwidth.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations.
Tradeoffs include average single-core speeds and poor small-model LLM text generation.
Well-suited for ARM-native multi-threaded applications, web serving, and databases.
